productRepo.head: ef44454 feat: add benben shadow service-entry harness

changeTarget.name: Feishu real-message test ingress for openclaw-benben shadow
exactProposedMode: Use a separate non-production Feishu test app/robot/callback surface for shadow validation.
Do not reuse the current production benben Feishu bot while old prod remains active.
Keep the current production benben bot bound to old prod until a separate production-cutover L3 gate.
Start with receive-only or dry-run delivery semantics unless a later gate explicitly authorizes test-surface reply delivery.
reason: Shadow synthetic service-entry validation has passed, but no real Feishu ingress is enabled.
The current production Feishu bot is tightly coupled to old prod and cannot safely be consumed by old and new services simultaneously.
A separate test surface allows real Feishu event verification without risking duplicate production consumption.

hardBlocksBeforeExecution: No execution is allowed until the user explicitly says: 进入修复阶段.
Do not enable the shadow service against the current production benben Feishu bot while openclaw-gateway.service still owns production.
Do not modify Feishu production callback, production bot credentials, old prod systemd, or old prod config in this test-ingress gate.
Do not send production Feishu messages from shadow.
Do not treat V2/V3/projection/transcript/raw archive as answer truth.

allowedPreflightReads: Confirm old prod and shadow service health and PIDs.
Confirm shadow feishu/telegram/qqbot channels and plugins are still disabled before any change.
Confirm the chosen Feishu test surface is separate from the current production benben bot using redacted identifiers or fingerprints only.
Confirm the product code path supports shadow-safe Feishu ingress with no production delivery by default.
candidateWrites: Create a rollback snapshot for any changed shadow config or workspace files.
Add only isolated test-surface Feishu configuration under the shadow config/state roots.
Enable only the minimum shadow test-ingress path required for the isolated test surface.
Restart or reload only openclaw-benben.service if the final config path requires it.
explicitlyOutOfScopeWrites: No current production benben bot credential rotation.
No openclaw-gateway.service restart, stop, or config change.
No provider/model network call unless separately authorized for the isolated test surface.

dualConsumerProofRequired: Show that the test Feishu surface has a different app/bot identity from the current production benben bot, using redacted fingerprints only.
Show that the current production benben bot remains served by old prod and is not configured in shadow for this gate.
Show that shadow Feishu config, if enabled, points only to the isolated test surface.
Show that old prod PID/health remains stable and that no duplicate response is emitted during a test-surface canary.
If a separate test surface cannot be confirmed, stop and do not proceed to real Feishu testing.
risk: Accidentally binding shadow to the production Feishu bot could create duplicate consumers or dropped replies.
A service reload/restart may interrupt the shadow instance even if old prod remains healthy.
Secret handling mistakes could leak Feishu credentials into logs, manifests, or git.
A real Feishu callback may write runtime state, consume model budget, or send a message if delivery guards are wrong.
External callback exposure may introduce auth, replay, or verification-token failure modes.
rollbackPlan: Restore shadow config/workspace files from the rollback snapshot created immediately before any write.
Disable the shadow Feishu test surface and set shadow feishu channel/plugin enabled flags back to false.
Remove or park the isolated test Feishu callback URL from the Feishu test app, if one was created.
Restart or reload only openclaw-benben.service if required to apply rollback.
Verify old prod openclaw-gateway.service remains active/running and still owns the production Feishu entrance.

preChange: Record old prod and shadow health/PID/ports.
Record redacted shadow channel/plugin state with feishu=false, telegram=false, qqbot=false.
Record redacted proof that the selected Feishu test surface is not the production benben bot.
Run local and shadow synthetic harness tests as baseline.
postChange: Confirm old prod health/PID/port remains unchanged.
Confirm shadow health/port remains ok after any reload.
Send exactly one canary message through the isolated Feishu test surface.
Confirm the canary reaches the intended shadow runtime path and is classified by the vNext envelope/identity/privacy kernels.
Confirm no production Feishu message was consumed by shadow.
Confirm delivery remains disabled or limited to the isolated test surface according to the final authorized gate.
Confirm no raw secret values, raw archive truth, or unsafe memory-admin action appears in logs or reports.
stopConditions: Production bot identity appears in the shadow Feishu config.
Old prod health or PID unexpectedly changes.
Shadow attempts to send to the production Feishu bot.
Any test event cannot be proven to come from the isolated test surface.
Any runtime action requires a higher-risk authorization than this gate grants.

authorizationStatus.reason: The latest user message only said continue. This manifest is local planning only; actual Feishu ingress changes still require the explicit phrase '进入修复阶段'.

nextLocalDevelopmentRecommendation: Before any Feishu live change, add or identify a shadow-safe real Feishu ingress adapter that can run receive-only/dry-run first.
Add tests that prove production ingress flags, delivery guards, model-call guards, and runtime-commit guards cannot be bypassed by Feishu-shaped payloads.
Prepare a separate execution manifest only after the isolated Feishu test surface is identified and explicitly authorized.
